Video.summary: The season-ending "Family Show" features the best variety show ensemble on television.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Best Of The Carol Burnett Show
Video.title: Family Show

Video.summary: Guest Shirley MacLaine joins Carol in sharing some of their fan mail.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Best Of The Carol Burnett Show
Video.title: Shirley MacLaine

Video.summary: Roddy McDowell & Ken Berry join in "High Hat", a spoof of Fred Astaire and Ginger Rogers movie musicals.
Video.title: Roddy McDowell, Ken Berry

Video.summary: Michele Lee headlines a wonderful production number, which also offers a visit with the 10th Street Family.
Video.title: Michele Lee

Video.summary: Jim Nabors is like Caine in "Kung Fu" in a spoof of the David Carradine TV series, here entitled "Yung Fool."
Video.title: Jim Nabors

Video.summary: Barrie Chase and Larry Hovis guest star! Chase provides a musical number, and Hovis plays "The Marriage Game."
Video.title: Larry Hovis, Barrie Chase

Video.summary: This "Family Show" features Dick as an airport traveller getting a long, awkward farewell from his family.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Best Of The Carol Burnett Show
Video.title: Family Show

Video.summary: Andy Griffith guests, retelling a familiar Aesop fable in his own inimitable fashion.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Best Of The Carol Burnett Show
Video.title: Andy Griffith

Video.summary: Ken Berry and Shirley Jones drop in to join in the fun!
Video.grandparentTitle: The Best Of The Carol Burnett Show
Video.title: Shirley Jones, Ken Berry

Video.summary: Guest star Steve Lawrence takes the stage to sing a duet with Dick.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Best Of The Carol Burnett Show
Video.title: Steve Lawrence

Video.summary: Vincent Price and Eydie Gorme star. Features an encounter on an elevator & the movie spoof "House Of Terror."
Video.title: Eydie Gorme, Vincent Price

Video.summary: Tim Conway stars as a German interrogator in WWII. Carol plays a housewife listening to an advice show.
Video.title: Tim Conway

Video.summary: Carol & Harvey are quarreling dolls in "Barby and Ben". Carol takes aim with spoof, "Rancid Harvest."
Video.title: Family Show

Video.summary: Mel Torme and Martha Raye guest. Featuring Martha and Carol as dog-lovers, and spoofs of Disney classics.
Video.title: Martha Raye, Mel Torme

Video.summary: Soupy Sales and Gloria Loring star. Loring spoofs "Valley Of The Dolls." Sales and Carol play newlyweds.
Video.title: Gloria Loring, Soupy Sales

Video.summary: Barbara McNair and Carol spoof a classic TV series with "I Try".
Video.grandparentTitle: The Best Of The Carol Burnett Show
Video.title: Barbara McNair, Sid Casesar

Video.summary: Guest John Byner plays both a villain and a trial witness. On "Carol & Sis", The Bradfords join a group.
Video.title: John Byner

Video.summary: The season finale features Carol putting the cast on the spot for Q&A, as well as beloved recurring sketches.
Video.title: Family Show

Video.summary: Madeline Kahn appears in the latest sketch with "The Family".
Video.grandparentTitle: The Best Of The Carol Burnett Show
Video.title: Madeline Kahn

Video.summary: John Byner & Helen Reddy join Carol in a sketch about swingers. Also, The Family plays a game of "Sorry!"
Video.title: John Byner, Helen Reddy

Video.summary: Highlights Dick Van Dyke as a TV announcer who fills in when technical difficulties scuttle the movie.
Video.title: Family Show

Video.summary: Steve Lawrence & Tim Conway star! Steve plays a talk show host, then solves a mystery in a spoof with Tim.
Video.title: Tim Conway, Steve Lawrence

Video.summary: Ken Berry stars with Carol as unsuccessful Western movie stars in "The Saga Of Lily And Billy."
Video.grandparentTitle: The Best Of The Carol Burnett Show
Video.title: Dionne Warwick, Ken Berry

Video.summary: Dick Van Dyke & Tony Randall star! Includes Dick as a salesman, and Carol & Tony as marrieds.
Video.title: Dick Van Dyke, Tony Randall

Video.summary: Carl Reiner appears in two sketches: as the husband of an accident-prone woman, and as a marriage counselor.
Video.title: Carl Reiner

Video.summary: Guests Ken Berry & Carl Reiner fly the funny skies in "Disaster '75!" And, one of Carol's classic sketches.
Video.title: Carl Reiner, Ken Berry

Video.summary: Guests Mama Cass Elliot and Ken Berry offer up some musical numbers and a series of commercial parodies.
Video.title: Ken Berry, Cass Elliot

Video.summary: Guests Ronnie Schell and Vicki Carr take to the stage with stand-up and song (respectively)!
Video.grandparentTitle: The Best Of The Carol Burnett Show
Video.title: Ronnie Schell, Vikki Carr

Video.summary: Lucille Ball and Mel Torme spoof the classic comedy "Some Like It Hot" with a gender-flipping twist.
Video.title: Lucille Ball, Mel Torme

Video.summary: Phil Silvers & Jean Stapleton guest! Features "The Old Folks", commercial parodies, and a musical number.
Video.title: Phil Silvers, Jean Stapleton

Video.summary: Steve Lawrence plays an unsuccessful comic. Also: commercial spoofs; Tim at war, attempting to fire a cannon.
Video.title: Steve Lawrence

Video.summary: Ken Berry and Totie Fields take part in Carol & Sis" and "As The Stomach Turns."
Video.grandparentTitle: The Best Of The Carol Burnett Show
Video.title: Ken Berry, Totie Fields

Video.summary: Tim Conway plays The Oldest Galley Slave, an ad exec visiting an actress, and a lawyer who is a rabbit.
Video.title: Tim Conway

Video.summary: Imogene Coca and Mel Torme guest. Coca and Carol are wives of rivals, and Torme is Igor in a Dracula sketch.
Video.title: Imogene Coca, Mel Torme

Video.summary: Jerry Lewis guests as an awkward groom set up for a society marriage. Also: another day with "The Old Folks."
Video.title: Jerry Lewis

Video.summary: Carol introduces the actors who dub voices for Spanish audiences, using a classic "Carol & Sis" sketch.
Video.title: Family Show

Video.summary: Guest Richard Chamberlain stars in "Gone With The Breeze". In "Carol & Sis", it's one sibling too many.
Video.title: Dick Chamberlain, Kay Medford
